prose:
**Ethylene frontier-orbital result** — Property: HOMO–LUMO frontier-orbital gap. Method used: DFT with tier `standard`, `b3lyp/def2-tzvp` as returned by chemkit/PySCF. Geometry: user-supplied fixed XYZ geometry from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz` (6 atoms); this was a frontier-orbitals/single-point electronic calculation at that geometry. Environment: gas phase (`solvent: null`). Charge 0, multiplicity 1. Software/program: `pyscf`.

Headline result: HOMO–LUMO gap = **7.4357165 eV**.

Returned orbital energies: HOMO = -7.6155818 eV; LUMO = -0.17986527 eV. Total electronic energy = -78.623486 hartree = -2139.4541 eV.

Convergence/integrity: SCF converged = true; SCF cycles = 6. Engine integrity trustworthy verdict = true (`status: ok`). No warnings were returned by the tool result. The chemkit result did not provide a live `.out` log path; the returned CLI invocation was: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/ethylene/ethylene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpgajfxaeg/live.json`.

Provenance: computed, not experimental.
